name: My claim says exposure wasn't over PEL—does that kill it?

mainEntity.name: My claim says exposure wasn't over PEL—does that kill it?

acceptedAnswer.description: No, OSHA requires evaluation for 'potential' overexposure regardless of PEL; symptoms prove hazard[1][3]. Comp needs causation, not just levels. - Use health effects evidence. Appeal with studies. PEL not sole factor.
